template.importantCLI: IMPORTANT: YOLOv5 uses CLI, not Python API
template.importantDetectron2: IMPORTANT: Detectron2 is more advanced but complex
template.importantSegmentation: IMPORTANT: Semantic segmentation with modern architectures
template.registerCOCO: Register your dataset in COCO format
template.finalLearningRate: Final learning rate (as fraction of lr0)
template.valPercentage: Validation percentage if no val split exists
template.executeCmds: Run these commands in your terminal
template.yoloUsesCli: YOLOv5 uses CLI, not Python API

tooltips.framework: Deep learning library to use. Adapts according to your project type.
tooltips.model: Model size: Nano is fast but less accurate, XLarge is slow but more precise.
tooltips.device: Where to train: CPU is slow but always works, GPU (CUDA) is very fast if you have NVIDIA.
tooltips.epochs: How many times the model sees the entire dataset. More epochs = more learning but more time.
tooltips.batch: How many images to process at once. Higher values use more memory but train faster.
tooltips.imgsz: Resolution of images during training. Larger = more detail but slower.
tooltips.seqLength: Number of time steps to use as input. E.g., use 50 past values to predict the future.
tooltips.forecastHorizon: How many future steps to predict (forecasting only). E.g., predict next 10 values.
tooltips.hiddenSize: Size of the neural network hidden layer (LSTM). Larger = more capacity but slower.
tooltips.optimizer: Algorithm that adjusts model weights during training. Adam: balanced and popular. AdamW: Adam with improved weight decay. SGD: classic, slower but sometimes better final result. RMSprop: good for RNNs.
tooltips.lr: How fast the model learns. High values = learns fast but can be unstable. 0.001 is a good starting point.
tooltips.patience: How many epochs to wait without improvement before stopping. If model doesn't improve in 50 epochs, stops automatically.
tooltips.valSplit: What percentage of your images to use for validation. 20% is standard: 80% trains, 20% validates.
tooltips.augmentationTitle: Techniques to create variations of your images and prevent the model from memorizing. Helps generalize better.
tooltips.augMosaic: Combines 4 random images into one. Very effective for improving small object detection.
tooltips.augMixup: Mixes two images with transparency. Helps model be more robust against occlusions.
tooltips.augHsv: Changes image colors (hue, saturation, brightness). Useful for different lighting conditions.
tooltips.augFlip: Flips images horizontally or vertically. Doubles your data effortlessly.
tooltips.augRotate: Rotates images slightly. Useful if your objects can appear at different angles.
tooltips.augScale: Zooms in/out and randomly crops. Simulates objects at different distances.
tooltips.metricsTitle: What information to save during training to analyze model performance.
tooltips.savePlots: Saves loss and accuracy plots during training. Very useful to see if the model is learning.
tooltips.saveConfMatrix: Table showing which classes are confused with each other. Ideal for understanding model errors.
tooltips.savePrCurves: Graphs showing the balance between precision and recall. Important for evaluating detection quality.
tooltips.savePredictions: Saves images with model predictions drawn on top. Perfect for visually seeing how well it works.
tooltips.saveMetricsCsv: Exports all metrics to a CSV file for analysis in Excel or creating your own graphs.
tooltips.exportTitle: Formats for deploying your trained model in production.
tooltips.exportOnnx: ONNX: universal format, works with TensorFlow, PyTorch, and almost any library. Ideal for production.
tooltips.exportTorchscript: TorchScript: PyTorch native format, very fast but only works with PyTorch.
tooltips.exportTflite: TensorFlow Lite: for mobile and embedded devices (Android, iOS, Raspberry Pi).
tooltips.exportOpenvino: OpenVINO: optimized for Intel CPUs. Very fast on Intel hardware.
tooltips.exportCoreml: CoreML: for native iOS/macOS apps. Takes advantage of Apple chips.
tooltips.exportTensorrt: TensorRT: maximum acceleration on NVIDIA GPUs. For high-performance production.

formats.yoloDetectionDesc: Bounding boxes in normalized YOLO format (.txt) with data.yaml configuration
formats.yoloSegmentationDesc: Polygons in YOLO segmentation format (.txt) with data.yaml configuration
formats.cocoDesc: COCO JSON format compatible with Mask R-CNN, RetinaNet, and Detectron2
formats.pascalVocDesc: Pascal VOC XML format with pixel coordinates (legacy format)
formats.csvDetectionDesc: CSV tabular format with bounding box coordinates
formats.csvClassificationDesc: CSV tabular format with image filename and class label
formats.csvKeypointsDesc: CSV tabular format with keypoint X, Y coordinates and visibility
formats.csvLandmarksDesc: CSV tabular format with landmark coordinates
formats.foldersByClassDesc: Images organized into separate folders by class name
formats.unetMasksDesc: PNG grayscale mask images for semantic segmentation training
formats.tixDesc: Export the entire project in a .tix file for backup or sharing

project.name: Project Name

classification.name: Simple Classification
classification.description: Assign a single label to each complete image
classification.useCases: Identify what the image contains (e.g., cat, dog, car)

bbox.name: Bounding Box
bbox.useCases: Detect and locate objects in images

multiLabel.name: Multi-Label Classification
multiLabel.description: Assign multiple labels to each image
multiLabel.useCases: Image with several elements (e.g., park + dog + sky)

detection.name: Object Detection
detection.description: Locate objects with rectangular boxes (bounding boxes)
detection.useCases: Find and locate specific objects in the image

segmentation.name: Semantic Segmentation
segmentation.description: Classify each pixel of the image by class

instanceSeg.name: Instance Segmentation
instanceSeg.useCases: Separate individual objects at pixel level

polygon.name: Polygon Segmentation
polygon.description: Draw polygons point-by-point for precise segmentation
polygon.useCases: Precise object boundaries, geometric shapes, lighter than masks

landmarks.name: Landmark Points
landmarks.useCases: Object centers, reference points, counting, locations

semanticSeg.name: Pure Semantic Segmentation
semanticSeg.description: Classify each pixel without distinguishing instances

ocr.name: OCR (Text Recognition)
ocr.description: Detect and recognize text in images

depthEstimation.name: Depth Estimation
depthEstimation.description: Predict distance of each pixel to the camera

soundEventDetection.name: Sound Event Detection
soundEventDetection.description: Identify and locate events in audio

musicGenreClassification.name: Music Genre Classification
musicGenreClassification.description: Identify musical genre of a song

voiceActivityDetection.name: Voice Activity Detection (VAD)
voiceActivityDetection.description: Detect when there is voice in audio

temporalActionLocalization.name: Temporal Action Localization
temporalActionLocalization.description: Detect when actions occur in video

videoSegmentation.name: Video Segmentation
videoSegmentation.description: Segment objects in video frame by frame

spatiotemporalAction.name: Spatiotemporal Action Detection
spatiotemporalAction.description: Detect actions in space and time

timeSeriesClassification.name: Time Series Classification
timeSeriesClassification.description: Assign a class to each complete time series (global classification mode)
timeSeriesClassification.useCases: Medical diagnosis, gesture recognition, ECG classification

timeSeriesForecasting.name: Time Series Forecasting
timeSeriesForecasting.description: Mark historical windows and prediction horizon using ranges

anomalyDetection.name: Anomaly Detection
anomalyDetection.description: Mark anomalous points in time series
anomalyDetection.useCases: Fraud detection, equipment failure, health monitoring

timeSeriesSegmentation.name: Time Series Segmentation
timeSeriesSegmentation.description: Divide series into segments with classes using ranges
timeSeriesSegmentation.useCases: Behavior analysis, regime change, activity states

patternRecognition.name: Pattern Recognition
patternRecognition.useCases: Stock analysis, biomedical signals, cycle detection

eventDetection.name: Event Detection
eventDetection.description: Mark discrete events in time using points
eventDetection.models: Change Point Detection, Event Detection CNN

timeSeriesRegression.name: Time Series Regression
timeSeriesRegression.description: Mark points with numeric target values
timeSeriesRegression.useCases: Demand estimation, dynamic pricing, value prediction

clustering.name: Series Clustering
clustering.description: Assign cluster to each complete time series (global classification mode)
clustering.useCases: Customer segmentation, pattern analysis, behavior grouping

imputation.name: Missing Value Imputation
imputation.description: Mark missing data sections to impute using ranges
imputation.useCases: Sensor data cleaning, filling measurement gaps

slamAnnotation.name: SLAM Annotation
slamAnnotation.description: Annotate data for localization and mapping

tour.welcome:
Welcome to Annotix! 🎯

A professional tool for annotating images and time series for machine learning model training. Supports multiple annotation types: Bounding Boxes, OBB, Segmentation, Keypoints, Polygons, Landmarks, Time Series and Classification.
tour.installation:
📦 Local installation

Annotix installs completely on your PC as a Progressive Web App (PWA). It doesn't require internet connection after initial installation. Works offline and runs directly from your browser.
tour.dataStorage:
💾 Data storage

IMPORTANT: All your data is saved in the browser's IndexedDB, not on a server. This means:
• ✅ Total privacy: your data never leaves your PC
• ✅ Works without internet
• ⚠️ Risk: if you clear browser data, everything will be lost
tour.projectBackup:
💼 Project backup

To avoid data loss:
• Export your projects in .tix format (contains everything: images, annotations, classes)
• You can reload the .tix file at any time
• Share .tix projects with other users
• Also export .tixconfig files to share only class configuration
tour.createProjectButton: Click New Project to begin. The creation modal will open for you to choose the project type.
tour.projectTypeSelection:
📋 Project types

In the modal you can choose from 8 different types:
• Bounding Box: Rectangular boxes (object detection)
• OBB: Oriented rotated boxes
• Mask: Pixel segmentation
• Keypoints: Key points (pose estimation)
• Polygon: Free-form polygons
• Landmarks: Facial points
• Time Series: Temporal series
• Classification: Image classification
tour.projectName:
✏️ Project name

In the modal, enter a descriptive name for your project. For example: 'Car Detection 2024' or 'Cell Segmentation'.

The project type will determine which tools are available in the canvas.
tour.classesPanel: This is the class management panel. Here you define the categories you'll annotate.
tour.classesInWorkflow:
🏷️ Class management

Classes can be added in two ways:

1. In the project creation modal: Define your main classes from the start

2. In the right panel (current): Add, edit or delete classes at any time during work

Each class has a unique name, a distinctive color and a sequential ID. Use number keys 1-9 to quickly select classes.
tour.gallery: The image gallery displays all images in your project. You can filter by all, annotated or unannotated. Thumbnails show the status of each image.
tour.loadImages: Press Load Images to import images to your project. Supports multiple formats: JPG, PNG, WEBP. You can also use Data Augmentation to automatically generate variations of your images.
tour.workspace: The main workspace where you visualize and annotate your images or time series. Use the mouse to draw, wheel to zoom, and hold middle button or use Pan tool to move the view.
tour.tools: The floating tools change according to project type. Include: Box (B), OBB (O), Mask (M), Select (V) and Pan (H). Each tool has specific controls like brush size for masks or zoom controls.
tour.stats: The statistics panel shows your project progress: total images, annotated images, total labels and number of classes. The progress bar helps you visualize how much work you've completed.
tour.exportButton: The Export button allows you to download your dataset. Let's see the available options...
tour.exportFormats:
📦 Export formats

In the export modal you'll find multiple options:

• YOLO Detection: .txt with normalized bounding boxes
• YOLO Segmentation: .txt with mask polygons
• YOLO OBB: .txt with oriented boxes
• COCO JSON: Standard COCO format
• U-Net Masks: PNG images for segmentation
• Pascal VOC XML: Pascal VOC format
• CSV: Data table
tour.trainingCode:
🐍 Training code

In the export modal, you'll find the option 'Generate Python training code'.

This creates a complete example script to train your model with YOLOv8 using Ultralytics:
• Automatic path configuration
• Training script with optimal hyperparameters
• Ready to run: just install dependencies and train
tour.shortcuts: Press the keyboard button or use F1 to see all available keyboard shortcuts. Include Ctrl+S to save, Ctrl+Z to undo, number keys to select classes, and more.
tour.finish:
Ready to start! 🚀

Remember:
• Save frequently with Ctrl+S
• Export backups in .tix format
• Your data is only in your browser

If you need help, press the ? button to see this tour again.

preprocessing.title: Preprocess Images
preprocessing.description: Images will be adjusted to a square size to optimize training. Choose the resizing strategy:

resize.name: Resize + Padding
resize.description: Scales down the image and adds padding (optimizes space and memory)

padding.name: Padding Only
padding.description: Keeps original size and adds padding (better quality)

preprocessing.paddingInfo: Symmetric black padding will be added

classification.singleHint: You can only select one label
classification.noImageSelected: No image selected. Please select an image from the gallery.
classification.multiLabelInstructions: Select one or more labels that apply to this image. Press Ctrl+S to save.
classification.singleLabelInstructions: Select the label that best describes this image. Press Ctrl+S to save.

augmentation.title: Data Augmentation
augmentation.variationsCountHelp: How many augmented images to create? (1-20)
augmentation.modeManualHelp: Apply the same configuration N times
augmentation.modeRandomHelp: Generate N variations with random values within specified ranges
augmentation.keepAnnotationsHelp: Bounding boxes will be automatically transformed with the image
augmentation.maskProjectWarningText: Annotations will NOT be preserved. You'll need to manually paint masks on each augmented image.

modal.title: 🚀 Install Annotix as an app
modal.androidInfo: Android: Works excellent. Full native installation, behaves like a Play Store app.
modal.iosInfo: iOS/iPadOS: Works well, but Safari may clear cache periodically. Important: Save your progress frequently by exporting your projects.
modal.linuxInfo: Linux: Works perfectly. Full support in Chrome, Firefox and Edge.
modal.windowsInfo: Windows: Should work without issues in Chrome and Edge, although we haven't tested it extensively.
modal.generalTip: 💡 Recommendation: Export your projects regularly as .tix backup, regardless of your operating system.

notifications.offline: Offline mode activated. App works without connection.

inference.title: Model Inference
inference.uploadHint: Supports YOLO Detection, Segmentation, and Classification
inference.noModelLoaded: No model loaded. Please upload a model first.
inference.viewModelInstructions: Download your model and open it in Netron to visualize the architecture.
inference.helpText: Upload a model with the format: projectname_v1.onnx to ensure compatibility. Use the brain button on each image to run inference. You can disable naming enforcement if you want to test other models.
inference.advancedVisualization: For advanced layer-by-layer visualization, use Netron:
inference.netronInstructionsText: Drag and drop your model file onto the Netron window below to see the complete layer-by-layer architecture.
inference.netronBrowserNote: Or drag your model file directly onto Netron below
inference.useNetron: Use the Interactive tab to view full architecture

training.title: Train Model
training.description: Train a YOLO model with your annotated dataset

env.description: Annotix needs Python with ultralytics to train models. Click below to set up automatically.
env.setupHint: This may take a few minutes on first setup

presets.title: Scenario Preset
presets.small_objectsDesc: Few classes, small objects, fast video. Optimized ROI with freeze.
presets.industrialDesc: Fixed camera inspection, defect detection. High precision, minimal augmentation.
presets.trafficDesc: Vehicles & pedestrians, many objects, scale variation. Dense scenes.
presets.edge_mobileDesc: Raspberry Pi, phones, IoT. Minimal size, maximum speed.
presets.medicalDesc: Lesions, cells, high precision. Conservative LR, heavy freeze.
presets.aerialDesc: Drone/satellite imagery, tiny objects, any orientation. High resolution.

result.description: Your model has been trained successfully.

backend.title: Training Backend
backend.description: Choose the framework for training your model
backend.installingPackagesDesc: This may take a few minutes. Your configuration will be preserved.

execution.title: Execution Mode
execution.description: Choose how to run the training
execution.localDesc: Run training locally using your GPU. Requires installed packages.
execution.downloadDesc: Download a ZIP with dataset, script and notebook to train elsewhere.

params.lr_encoderDesc: Learning rate for the encoder backbone
params.grad_accum_stepsDesc: Number of steps to accumulate gradients
params.early_stoppingDesc: Stop training when validation loss plateaus
params.gradient_checkpointingDesc: Trade compute for memory — enables larger models
params.warmup_itersDesc: Number of warmup iterations before full LR
params.epochsDesc: Total passes over dataset. More = better convergence but slower.
params.batchSizeDesc: -1 for auto (60% GPU memory). Larger = more stable gradients.
params.imgszDesc: Input resolution. Quadratic impact on speed. Match to object size.
params.patienceDesc: Epochs without improvement before early stopping.
params.max_detDesc: Maximum detections per image. Lower = faster post-processing.
params.multi_scaleDesc: Random size variation per batch. Improves scale robustness.
params.rectDesc: Minimal padding respecting aspect ratio. More efficient for non-square images.
params.cacheDesc: Cache images in RAM (fast) or disk. Speeds up training significantly.
params.optimizerDesc: Auto selects the best optimizer for your YOLO version.
params.lr0Desc: Initial learning rate. Higher = faster but can be unstable.
params.lrfDesc: Final LR as fraction of initial: lr_final = lr0 × lrf.
params.cos_lrDesc: Cosine scheduler: smooth decay, better fine convergence than linear.
params.warmup_epochsDesc: Epochs where LR gradually ramps up from near-zero to lr0.
params.momentumDesc: SGD momentum or Adam beta1. Higher = smoother training.
params.weight_decayDesc: L2 regularization. Higher = stronger regularization against overfitting.
params.boxDesc: Localization loss weight. Increase for precise bounding boxes.
params.clsDesc: Classification loss weight. Increase with few classes.
params.dflDesc: Distribution Focal Loss. Set to 0 for YOLO26.
params.pretrainedDesc: Use COCO pretrained weights. Always recommended unless dataset is very large.
params.freezeDesc: Freeze first N layers (backbone). 0 = train all. Higher with small datasets.
params.ampDesc: FP16/FP32 mixed precision. Faster and less memory. Disable only if NaN errors.
params.nbsDesc: Nominal batch size for loss normalization. Rarely needs adjustment.
params.single_clsDesc: Treat all classes as one. For binary detection (object/no object).
params.warmup_bias_lrDesc: Learning rate for bias parameters during warmup.
params.valSplitDesc: Fraction of images for validation. 0.2 = 80% train, 20% val.
params.workersDesc: Data loading threads. More = faster loading but more CPU/RAM.
params.deviceDesc: Compute device. Auto detects the best available.
params.mosaicDesc: Combines 4 images. Great for dense scenes. Reduce with small objects.
params.close_mosaicDesc: Disable mosaic for last N epochs. Stabilizes final training phase.
params.mixupDesc: Blends two images with transparency. Light regularization.
params.translateDesc: Random horizontal/vertical shift as fraction of image size.
params.scaleDesc: Random zoom variation. Increase if objects vary in apparent size.
params.fliplrDesc: Horizontal flip probability. Disable if laterality matters.
params.flipudDesc: Vertical flip probability. Enable for aerial/microscopy images.
params.degreesDesc: Random rotation range in degrees. 180 for aerial (any orientation).
params.shearDesc: Shear deformation in degrees. Use sparingly.
params.hsv_hDesc: Color hue variation. Keep low if colors are diagnostically relevant.
params.hsv_sDesc: Saturation variation. Reduce for controlled lighting.
params.hsv_vDesc: Brightness variation. Increase for variable lighting conditions.
params.erasingDesc: Random erasing simulates occlusion. Reduce with small objects.

model.sizeDescNano: Fastest, lower accuracy. Good for edge devices.

training.fineTuneDesc: Continue training from a previously trained model

setup.description: Choose a folder where your projects will be stored. Each project will be a subfolder with its images, annotations and configuration.

video.fpsHelp: Lower values = fewer frames, faster. Recommended: 2-10 FPS
video.noTracks: No tracks. Create one to start tracking.
video.confirmDeleteVideo: Delete this video and all its frames?
video.bakeDesc: Writes track interpolations as annotations on each frame
